calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-15-2011 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-18-2011 Continued To Mar-15-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Williams for Anthony Johnson.

The Court informed Mr. Williams that he had to send notice of the June 1, 2010 First Amended Accounting and Petition for Final Distribution to all Beneficiaries and stated that the notice has to indicate the title of the above petition. The matter was continued to March 15, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-24-2010 Continued To Jan-18-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Williams for Anthony Johnson.

The Court addressed the bond issue and the lack of notice to all individuals named as recipients of specific gifts and stated that it had received the notice of withdrawal. The Court also stated that there needs to be a notice of hearing to all heirs that received gifts, that it saw the notice and that the letter isn't sufficient, that all individuals entitled to gifts should be given notice, and that notice should be on the Judicial Council form. Mr. Williams stated that they sent notice, that they didn't know that there were joint accounts, that the information wasn't forthcoming, that the decedent wasn't in debt at the time of her death, and that she only owed elder care and Wells Fargo. The Court stated that notice needs to be given and that once that is done then the Estate can close. The Court continued the matter to January 18, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Williams stated that he would try to obtain the information from the will and would contact the Executor.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR#11216. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: none present.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-03-2010 Continued To Nov-24-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-22-2010 Continued To Nov-03-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Williams for Anthony Johnson (Executor).

Mr. Williams stated that he sent a letter to the title company directing them not to distribute his fees and that the title company sent him said fees in January. In addition, Mr. Williams explained the extent of services provided to support his fees, stated that the Executor was in Florida, that the Executor was not returning, that he (Mr. Williams) would not continue to pay the bond premiums and requested discharge of the bond. The Court stated that it was not willing to discharge the bond. The Court also addressed the issue of fees paid being above the value of the statutory fees without a court order, questioned the jewelery that was appraised by the Probate Referee and stated that all individuals need notice of the specific gifts. The matter was continued to November 3, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-25-2010 Continued To Sep-22-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-21-2010 Continued To Aug-25-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-12-2010 Continued To Jul-21-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Williams for Anthony Johnson (Executor).

The Court stated that the issues raised in the Examiner's notes were not addressed and that an amendment had not been filed. Mr. Williams stated that he had the amended petition but that he had not filed it due to concern with having to pay additional filing fees. In addition, Mr. Williams stated that the Executor sold the property without instructing him, that the withholding and fees had been paid, that the bank accounts had been changed by the Decedent after he drew up the estate documents and that the Decedent also gave away her jewelery. The matter was continued to July 21, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of the amended petition.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-14-2010 Continued To May-12-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
